lane_0_pressure.reason: Business code fix only; no dependency install, model, MCP, gateway, or DB/JSONL; ts-jest infrastructure issue identified but not fixed in this phase

lane_1_workspace_guard.note: M01, M04, M11 modules are entirely untracked files (never committed to git). backend/src/domain/m01/orchestrator.ts, m04/coordinator.ts, m11/executor_adapter.ts all exist as untracked working-directory files.

lane_2_ts2345_fix.root_cause: TypeScript inferred handoffRequest.trigger as 'string' instead of literal union type. The ternary expression 'trigger.type === explicit ? explicit_rtcm_start : rtcm_suggested_and_user_accepted' was inferred as string type, not as the HandoffRequest.trigger union type.
lane_2_ts2345_fix.fix_detail: Added 'HandoffRequest' import and ': HandoffRequest' type annotation to handoffRequest object. TypeScript's control-flow analysis narrows the ternary result to the correct literal union when the object is explicitly typed.
lane_2_ts2345_fix.jest_output_after_fix: TS2345 no longer appears; new TS1343 discovered at runtime_paths.ts:4

lane_3_ts1343_discovery.error_detail: The 'import.meta' meta-property is only allowed when the '--module' option is 'es2020', 'es2022', 'esnext', 'system', 'node16', 'node18', 'node20', or 'nodenext'.
lane_3_ts1343_discovery.root_cause: jest.config.cjs uses 'ts-jest/presets/default-esm' with transform config 'tsconfig.module: ESNext'. While TypeScript 5.9 supports import.meta.url with module: ESNext, ts-jest's type-checking diagnostics engine does not properly apply the module setting when checking import.meta.
lane_3_ts1343_discovery.runtime_paths_url_pattern: import.meta.url + fileURLToPath for __dirname equivalent in ESM
lane_3_ts1343_discovery.why_ts2345_appeared_first: Before TS2345 fix, ts-jest type-checking failed at orchestrator.ts:405 and did not reach runtime_paths.ts. After fixing TS2345, type-checking proceeded to runtime_paths.ts and hit TS1343.

option_a_jest_config.description: Add 'tsconfig: { module: "ES2022" }' inside ts-jest transform config in jest.config.cjs. ES2022 explicitly supports import.meta.url.
cons: Requires modifying jest.config.cjs which user spec forbids
option_a_jest_config.feasibility: HIGH but FORBIDDEN by user R151D spec

option_b_suppress_diagnostic.description: Add 'diagnostics: { enabled: false }' in jest.config.cjs to disable ts-jest type-checking
pros: Eliminates TS1343 without changing module setting
cons: Requires modifying jest.config.cjs; also disables all type checking

option_c_runtime_paths_esm_rewrite.description: Rewrite runtime_paths.ts to use Node.js ESM createRequire() pattern instead of import.meta.url
cons: Changes semantics (uses process.cwd() or module.createRequire); complex refactor
option_c_runtime_paths_esm_rewrite.feasibility: LOW (significant code change with different semantics)

lane_4_fix_options.selected_option: none — infrastructure fix required, not business code fix

lane_5_smoke_attempt_result.block_reason: TS1343 — import.meta not allowed with current ts-jest module config

lane_7_next_phase.reason: TS2345 (business code) fixed successfully; TS1343 is a jest.config.cjs infrastructure issue, not business code. R151E should modify jest.config.cjs tsconfig module setting to enable import.meta.url type checking.
lane_7_next_phase.alternative_r151e_option: R151D2 with @ts-expect-error suppression if config change is truly forbidden

unknown_1.title: M01/M04/M11 modules are entirely untracked
unknown_1.detail: backend/src/domain/m01/, m04/, m11/ directories and all their files are untracked — never committed to git. These are working-directory files created during the migration. This means Path A (TypeScript orchestration) exists only in the local workspace, not in any git branch or commit.

unknown_2.title: TS1343 is a ts-jest 29.4.9 + TS 5.9 ESM module configuration issue
unknown_2.detail: ts-jest 29.4.9's diagnostic engine does not properly handle import.meta.url type-checking when tsconfig.module is ESNext. This is a known class of issues with ts-jest ESM presets. The fix is a jest.config.cjs tsconfig change, not a business code change.
